standard_variable.semantic_intent: source-recorded urinalysis protein ordinal result event
standard_variable.semantic_grain: one retained source-recorded urinalysis protein result event

database_mapping.source_grain: one raw hosp.labevents row for itemid 51492 or 51992 before approved result normalization
database_mapping.target_grain: one retained interpretable urinalysis protein ordinal result event row

ordinal_result_build_translation.target_entity_grain: laboratory result event with best-available admission and ICU-stay linkage
ordinal_result_build_translation.result_normalization_translation: normalize approved negative, trace, and semiquantitative mg/dL dipstick variants into negative, trace, mild_positive, moderate_positive, marked_positive, or severe_positive; blank placeholders and artifact numeric variants are excluded
ordinal_result_build_translation.raw_result_retention_translation: retain raw source value, raw numeric value, raw itemid, source label, specimen id, raw unit, abnormal flag, hadm recovery, and stay linkage status
ordinal_result_build_translation.no_row_translation: absence of a retained row means no interpretable protein urinalysis result under this source rule, not a negative protein result

validation_contract.no_row_policy_check: absence of row is not universal proof of negative protein or no kidney disease
notes: This is a Class 6 ordinal/text result event, not a quantitative urine-protein measurement and not a kidney phenotype.
Hospital-only and insufficient-linkage rows are retained with stay_link_status for downstream filtering.

execution_contract.formal_output_rule: The governed execution.py entrypoint should read this mapping spec and delegate to the current local reviewed implementation.
execution_contract.non_bypassable_target_rule: Formal std_protein_urinalysis_result outputs for MIMIC-IV-3.1 should be produced only through the governed execution.py path.
